Exam_Format: Individual written computer exam (EC) plus group written lab report (pass/fail).
Summary: Intro to bioinformatics workflows for molecular data, with emphasis on Markov models, evolutionary analysis, and network biology.
Key_Topics: Basics of molecular biology and genetics; Hidden Markov models and sequence analysis; Sequence similarity and alignment; Phylogeny reconstruction; Quantitative trait modelling; Microarray analysis; Network biology
Notes: Lectures and computer exercises; labs share the 3 ECTS practical component.

Exam_Format: Individual written exam (EC) plus mandatory computer labs (pass/fail).
Summary: Covers design and implementation of relational databases, from conceptual modelling to indexing, concurrency, and recovery.
Key_Topics: Storage and retrieval in DBMS; EER modelling; Relational databases and SQL algebra; Indexing and file structures; Transactions and concurrency control; Recovery and backup; Heuristic query optimisation
Notes: Labs provide half of the credits; theory-heavy written exam controls the grade.

Exam_Format: Single written examination (6 ECTS) covering all outcomes.
Summary: Provides the theoretical probability foundation needed for advanced statistics, focusing on distributions, transforms, and convergence results.
Key_Topics: Univariate and multivariate probability distributions; Expectations, variance, moments; Joint and conditional distributions, independence; Bayesian elements; Moment generating functions and other transforms; Order statistics; Multivariate normal distribution; Modes of stochastic convergence and limit theorems
Notes: No labs or projects; pure theory assessed via written exam (TENT 6 credits).

Exam_Format: Individual written project report plus final oral examination of the project work.
Summary: Independent applied research task in data mining or machine learning, tied to an ongoing project with supervisor guidance.
Key_Topics: Problem scoping within data mining or ML; Literature and context study; Method selection and application; Data analysis and experimentation; Reporting and presentation of findings
Notes: All 6 credits come from the project deliverables; strong oral component during the defense.

Exam_Format: Group lab reports (3 ECTS) plus individual written computer exam (3 ECTS).
Summary: Blends classical and modern sequence modelling, giving hands-on practice with forecasting and inference for sequential data.
Key_Topics: Linear autoregressive models; Nonlinear autoregressive models incl. temporal CNNs; State-space models, Kalman filtering, smoothing; Nonlinear state-space and Sequential Monte Carlo filtering; Recurrent neural networks; Model estimation, validation, forecasting
Notes: Computer labs emphasise implementation and forecasting accuracy.

Exam_Format: Group-written and oral lab presentations (3 ECTS) plus an individual written project report (3 ECTS).
Summary: Introduces quantitative text analytics from data acquisition to predictive modelling, emphasising decision making from text.
Key_Topics: Information retrieval; Language technology preprocessing; Predictive modelling and text classification; Text clustering and topic modelling; Information extraction; Validation methods and evaluation
Notes: Labs require oral presentations; final grade driven by the solo project report.

Exam_Format: Individual written examination (5 ECTS) plus lab reports and seminar participation (1 ECTS).
Summary: Covers theory and applications of multivariate models for inference, dimension reduction, and hypothesis testing.
Key_Topics: Matrix algebra for random vectors; Multivariate normal distribution and sampling distributions; Inference on mean vectors and covariance structures; Principal component analysis; Factor analysis; Canonical correlation analysis; MANOVA models
Notes: Seminars discuss lab assignments and research use-cases.

Exam_Format: Written reports on computer assignments (1 ECTS) plus active seminars and a final written exam (5 ECTS).
Summary: Advanced Bayesian machine learning focusing on probabilistic modelling, graphical models, and sequential inference techniques.
Key_Topics: Bayesian learning framework (likelihood, prior, posterior); Generative vs discriminative models; Gaussian processes; State-space models; Kalman filtering and smoothing; Particle methods; Markov and hidden Markov models; Graphical models including Bayesian networks and Markov random fields
Notes: Seminars require presenting computer assignments; exam controls majority of credits.

Exam_Format: Written reports on computer exercises, seminar participation, research article presentation, plus a final written or oral exam (5 ECTS).
Summary: Explores static, interactive, and animated visualization techniques for complex data, including critique of misleading graphics.
Key_Topics: Principles of effective and misleading visualization; Univariate and bivariate graphics (histograms, bar charts, scatter/time plots); Text visualization (word trees/clouds); Multidimensional tools (scatter matrices, treemaps, heatmaps, bubble plots, Chernoff faces, star charts, parallel coordinates); Multidimensional scaling; Geovisualization via web apps; Animations and interactive techniques (linked graphs, brushing, guided tours); Publication quality graphics
Notes: Final assessment can be written or oral depending on offering; seminars focus on recent visualization research.

Exam_Format: Compulsory computer assignments (LAB1, 1 ECTS) plus a final written examination (TEN1, 5 ECTS).
Summary: Introduces probabilistic modelling of causal networks with Bayesian networks, covering inference algorithms and engineering applications.
Key_Topics: Bayesian paradigm and Dirichlet modelling; Conditional independence and Bayesian networks; Evidence handling and update methods; Graph decomposability and structure learning; Exponential families and conditional Gaussian models; Causality and Pearl’s intervention calculus; Junction trees and message passing; Factor graphs and sum-product algorithm
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Graded problem-solving assignments (UPG2, 6 ECTS) assessed on the LiU 3–5 scale.
Summary: Covers foundational group, ring and field theory needed for advanced mathematics and computing applications, focusing on structure theorems and algebraic constructions.
Key_Topics: Cyclic and finite abelian groups; Quotient groups and group actions; Ring ideals, homomorphisms and principal ideal domains; Polynomial rings and isomorphism theorems; Algebraic and transcendental field extensions; Splitting and fraction fields; Finite fields and Frobenius homomorphism
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Laboratory exercises (LAB1, 1.5 ECTS), participation in seminars (BAS1, 1 ECTS) and graded essay assignments (HEM1, 3.5 ECTS) form the assessment.
Summary: Explores how information systems support healthcare, from literature retrieval and medical records to ontologies, data modelling and terminology standards.
Key_Topics: Indexing and retrieval of medical information; Information quality metrics and MEDLINE/MeSH usage; Source evaluation and ER modelling; Database structure for patient records; Security and confidentiality of clinical data; Medical terminology concepts, coding and classification; Ontology construction and interoperability standards
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Assessment combines a graded group project (PRA1, 6 ECTS), digital written exam on theory (DIT1, 4 ECTS) and tool-focused labs (LAB2, 2 ECTS); optional hand-in (UPG1) supports learning.
Summary: Large-scale software engineering course combining lectures and a semester-long project to practice requirements, architecture, iterative development and quality assurance.
Key_Topics: Requirements engineering and software quality; Project planning and development processes; Software design and architecture; Testing strategies and quality assurance; Configuration management and industry-grade tooling; Iterative project execution with customer interaction
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Single laboratory-based examination (LAB2, 6 ECTS) graded on the 3–5 scale, covering planning, execution and reporting of tests.
Summary: Focuses on practical and theoretical aspects of software testing across unit, integration and system levels, including automated techniques and industry tools.
Key_Topics: Test concepts at unit, module and system level; Automated versus manual test data generation; Static and dynamic analysis; Test-driven development and model-based testing; Industry and academic guest lectures on tools and techniques
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Laboratory work in Prolog and constraint programming (LAB1, 2 ECTS) plus a written exam covering theory and techniques (TEN1, 4 ECTS).
Summary: Explores declarative and logic programming with Prolog, connecting logical semantics to constraint solving and database/grammar applications.
Key_Topics: Declarative versus operational semantics; Negation as failure and the cut; Prolog programming idioms; Logic with databases and grammars; Search strategies and constraint logic programming
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Course_Name: Design and Programming of Computer Games
Exam_Format: Single graded game-development project (PRA2, 6 ECTS) including design analysis, prototype implementation and presentation.
Summary: Examines how design parameters shape digital game experiences and how to prototype gameplay with high-level development tools.
Key_Topics: Characteristics of computer games and player experience; Game design parameters and analysis; Use of high-level languages and engines; Prototype implementation and evaluation workflows
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Five-hour computer exam (DAT3, 6 ECTS) with optional preparatory lab tests (DAK1-3) that credit exam sections.
Summary: Deep dive into advanced C++ constructs, covering templates, type semantics and modern standard library patterns for building efficient modules.
Key_Topics: Advanced type system and value categories; Object lifetime and memory management; Operator overloading and namespaces; Template metaprogramming and static constructs; Standard library design, function objects and C++ idioms
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Laboratory assignments (LAB1, 3 ECTS) plus a written exam (TEN1, 3 ECTS) on theory and modelling techniques.
Summary: Covers retrieval and storage techniques for text, semi-structured data and knowledge bases, including XML/XQuery, NoSQL models and ontologies.
Key_Topics: Information retrieval models and evaluation; XML/RDF representation of semi-structured data; XQuery and XML Schema design; NoSQL database principles; Ontologies, OWL and knowledge-base querying; Data integration challenges
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Computer-based labs (LAB1, 1 ECTS) plus a digital exam (DAT2, 5 ECTS) that tests theoretical and problem-solving mastery.
Summary: Introduces Bayesian perspectives on advanced model classes—graphical models, HMMs, reinforcement learning and Gaussian processes—for solving complex ML tasks.
Key_Topics: Bayesian and Markov graphical models; Hidden Markov models with forward/backward and Viterbi algorithms; Reinforcement learning incl. Q-learning and REINFORCE; Gaussian processes for regression/classification
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Course_Name: Advanced Project Course - AI and Machine Learning
Exam_Format: Project work (PRA1, 6 ECTS) assessed on technical results, written documentation and oral presentation.
Summary: Team-based applied AI/ML projects tied to research or industry clients, focusing on requirements analysis, implementation and delivery of intelligent systems.
Key_Topics: Problem analysis and requirements for AI/ML systems; Application of machine learning methods to real data; Project planning and management; Technical documentation and presentation for stakeholders
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Course_Name: Advanced Project Course: Secure Distributed and Embedded Systems
Exam_Format: Project work (PRA1, 6 ECTS) graded on technical solution quality, written documentation and oral presentation.
Summary: Client-driven projects in secure distributed and embedded systems, applying advanced networking, security and embedded knowledge to real problems.
Key_Topics: Problem analysis in networks, distributed systems and security; Application of embedded/distributed design methods; Requirements engineering and project management; Technical reporting and presentation for clients
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Exam_Format: Computer-based exam (DAT1, 2 ECTS), design labs (LAB1, 3 ECTS) and graded assignment (UPG1, 1 ECTS).
Summary: Strengthens large-scale software design skills through refactoring, metaprogramming, framework/API design and advanced debugging.
Key_Topics: History and application of design patterns; Refactoring strategies; Code generation and DSL design; Metaprogramming and dependency injection; Framework/API design and advanced debugging/memory profiling
Notes: Data imported from Studieinfo (Course description, exam breakdown, topics)

Course_Name: Programming Project with Open Source Code
Exam_Format: PRA1 project (6 ECTS) with oral individual review of Git-based contributions and documentation.
Summary: Semester-long contribution to an external open-source project focusing on either large-scale engineering workflows or advanced algorithmic features with team coaching.
Key_Topics: Open-source contribution workflows; Planning and monitoring shared project plans; Communication with upstream communities; Large-scale software engineering practices; Advanced algorithmic contributions
Notes: Spans periods 1-2 in module 4; oral defense is part of the project assessment.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written exam (3 ECTS) plus LAB1 labs (1 ECTS) and UPG1 project work (2 ECTS).
Summary: Designs and analyses wireless networks over shared media, treating MAC protocols, latency/freshness metrics and emerging 5G-and-beyond paradigms.
Key_Topics: Shared wireless medium fundamentals; Random access and MAC slicing; Wireless information-theoretic limits; Latency and information freshness; Large-scale wireless networking; Communications for distributed ML/CPS/non-terrestrial networks
Notes: Period 1, module 2 with guest lectures from industry.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written exam (3 ECTS) and LAB1 network labs (3 ECTS); UPG1 is voluntary.
Summary: Applied introduction to Internet architecture and protocols from application to link layers, with labs implementing custom protocols and traffic analysis.
Key_Topics: Protocol layering and reference models; MAC protocols and LANs; TCP/IP routing, congestion and flow control; IPv6, NAT and Mobile IP; Wireless networks (Bluetooth/WiFi/WiMAX); Internet applications (DNS, web, VoIP); Introductory network security
Notes: Period 1, module 1.

Exam_Format: TEN1 final exam (4 ECTS) plus LAB1 architecture labs (2 ECTS).
Summary: Covers advanced CPU/system architecture topics such as pipelining, superscalar/VLIW execution, memory hierarchies and multiprocessors to analyze modern platforms.
Key_Topics: Instruction set and microarchitecture design; Memory hierarchy and management; Pipelining and branch prediction; Superscalar/VLIW/out-of-order execution; I/O subsystems and buses; Multiprocessor and parallel architectures
Notes: Module 2 offering typically runs first study period.

Exam_Format: UPG1 project with oral and written presentation (6 ECTS).
Summary: Physically based rendering course covering optics, Monte Carlo integration, path tracing and photon mapping supported by seminars and a rendering project.
Key_Topics: Optics and light transport; Ray tracing algorithms; Monte Carlo sampling/integration; Rendering equation strategies; Stochastic path tracing; Photon mapping and caustics; Perception and display considerations
Notes: Period 1, module 4 at Campus Norrköping.

Exam_Format: MUN1 oral exam (3 ECTS) plus LAB1 scientific-visualization labs (3 ECTS).
Summary: Gives deep insights into visualization of scalar, vector and tensor data, from pipeline basics to volume rendering and application-specific techniques.
Key_Topics: Visualization pipeline and data representation; Scalar/vector/tensor visualization techniques; Volume rendering; Data exploration and feature extraction; Topological methods; Application-specific visualization
Notes: Period 1, module 3 at Campus Norrköping.

Exam_Format: PRA2 project (4 ECTS) + LAB2 labs (1.5 ECTS) + UPG2 graded assignment (0.5 ECTS).
Summary: Project-driven media production course for immersive environments (HMDs, domes, curved screens) blending stereo video, immersive audio and realtime graphics.
Key_Topics: Immersive display technologies and constraints; Stereoscopic video capture; Immersive audio production; Realtime graphics integration; Content design/workflow for VR/dome media
Notes: Listed for periods 1-2, module 2→1; HT2026 instances currently marked CANCELLED on Studieinfo—confirm with programme office.

Course_Name: Artificial Intelligence for Interactive Media, Project
Exam_Format: PRA1 project (6 ECTS) with oral and written presentation of AI-for-media application.
Summary: Explores AI techniques for interactive media by implementing and presenting a self-defined project using approaches such as behavioral animation, RL and generative AI.
Key_Topics: Intelligent exploration of virtual worlds; Artificial life and behavioral animation; Reinforcement/deep learning; NLP/chatbots; Generative AI; Genetic algorithms and swarm intelligence; Emotion-based decision making
Notes: Period 1, module 2 at Campus Norrköping.

Exam_Format: TEN2 written exam (4 ECTS) plus LAB2 assignment report (2 ECTS).
Summary: Analytical course on multiple-access networks, stochastic traffic models and routing, using assignments to apply Markov/Poisson models.
Key_Topics: OSI layering; Slotted multiple-access and Markov models; Poisson arrivals; ALOHA and stabilisation; Splitting algorithms; CSMA/CD/CA and IEEE 802.11; Packet radio networks; Spanning-tree and shortest-path routing
Notes: Period 1, module 3.

Exam_Format: TEN2 written exam (4 ECTS) plus LAB1 cryptology labs (2 ECTS).
Summary: Treats theoretical and applied cryptography, from stream/block ciphers to asymmetric protocols, hash functions and quantum-aware methods with lab implementations.
Key_Topics: Perfect secrecy and randomness; Stream and block ciphers; Modes of operation and introductory cryptanalysis; RSA and public-key distribution; Digital signatures and hash functions; Quantum/post-quantum and zero-knowledge protocols
Notes: Period 1, module 2.

Exam_Format: UPG1 written hand-ins (2 ECTS) plus UPG2 seminar participation (4 ECTS).
Summary: Prepares for the master’s thesis via literature reviews, method selection, academic writing and ethical reflection tailored to each student’s topic area.
Key_Topics: Quantitative and qualitative scientific methods; Scientific writing standards; Literature search and reference management; Critical analysis of reliability/validity; Research ethics and societal impact
Notes: Period 2, module 3 at Campus Norrköping; cannot be combined with TDDD89 in the degree.

Course_Name: Technical, Economic and Societal Evaluation of IT-products
Exam_Format: UPG5 individual graded assignments (4 ECTS) plus UPG4 group project (2 ECTS).
Summary: Evaluates IT ventures from technical, economic and socio-technical perspectives via literature-driven seminars and a holistic project report.
Key_Topics: Evaluation models for IT products; Socio-technical and economic impact analysis; Incentives for technology adoption; Literature seminars; Project-based assessments of real IT ventures
Notes: Period 2, module 4.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written security exam (3 ECTS) plus UPG1 labs/assignments (3 ECTS).
Summary: Focuses on vulnerability discovery, secure design and defensive programming for C/C++ and web software using lab assignments and a theory exam.
Key_Topics: Vulnerability analysis and exploits; Attack/vulnerability modeling; Secure requirements and design; Secure coding principles; Intrusion prevention mechanisms; Security testing workflows
Notes: Period 2, module 1.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written exam (4 ECTS) and LAB1 real-time labs (2 ECTS).
Summary: Introduces real-time scheduling, resource sharing, RTOS concepts, fault tolerance and QoS-aware networking for dependable systems.
Key_Topics: Static and dynamic scheduling (cyclic, RM, EDF); Resource sharing and ceiling protocols; Real-time operating systems; Dependability and fault tolerance; QoS in networked RTS (IntServ/DiffServ); Distributed RT communication (TTP/CAN)
Notes: Period 2, module 4.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written exam (3 ECTS) plus LAB1 multicore/GPU labs (3 ECTS).
Summary: Teaches how to parallelize applications for multicore CPUs and GPUs, covering algorithm design, threading, SIMD and CUDA/OpenCL programming.
Key_Topics: Multicore/many-core concepts; Theory of parallelization; Parallel algorithm design; Thread programming; SIMD/data-parallel techniques; CUDA/OpenCL programming; Synchronization and scheduling issues
Notes: Period 2, module 2.

Exam_Format: DAT2 computer-based exam (4 ECTS) and LAB2 programming labs (2 ECTS).
Summary: Probabilistic introduction to machine learning for regression and classification, linking theory to implementation via computer labs.
Key_Topics: Supervised vs unsupervised learning; Discriminative and generative models; k-NN and Bayesian classifiers; Regression and regularization (ridge/lasso); Model selection and cross-validation; PCA/ICA; SVMs and decision trees; Gaussian processes and mixture models
Notes: Period 2, module 1.

Exam_Format: UPG1 graded assignments (4 ECTS) plus LAB1 multi-agent labs (2 ECTS).
Summary: Surveys multi-agent system architectures, LLM-based agents, distributed constraint solving, game theory and auctions with implementation labs.
Key_Topics: Multi-agent architectures; LLM-based agent systems; Distributed constraint satisfaction/optimization; Game theory and auctions; Agent communication and speech acts; Preference aggregation and voting; MDP-based multi-agent decision making
Notes: Period 2, module 1.

Exam_Format: LAB1 Python labs (3 ECTS) and PRA1 individual project (3 ECTS).
Summary: Hands-on text mining workflow from information retrieval and NLP preprocessing to modelling, clustering, topic modelling and information extraction.
Key_Topics: Information retrieval; NLP preprocessing; Text classification; Text clustering and topic modelling; Information extraction; Validation methods; Project-based text-mining applications
Notes: Period 2, module 2.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written exam (3 ECTS) plus LAB1 compiler-construction labs (3 ECTS).
Summary: Comprehensive compiler design course from lexical/syntax analysis to semantic analysis, optimization and code generation while building a compiler.
Key_Topics: Translators and compiler structure; Lexing and parsing; Semantic analysis and symbol management; Intermediate representations and memory management; RISC-focused code generation/optimization; Error handling; Compiler tools and generators
Notes: Period 2, module 1.

Exam_Format: LAB1 procedural graphics labs (3 ECTS) plus UPG1 project (3 ECTS) with written/oral reporting.
Summary: Explores procedural texturing and geometry synthesis techniques—noise, fractals, turbulence and shader programming—for realistic imagery.
Key_Topics: Procedural texturing principles; 2D/3D/4D textures; Random generators and Perlin/lattice noise; Fractal Brownian motion/turbulence; Splines and Fourier synthesis; Shader-based texture/geometry synthesis; Introductory deep learning methods
Notes: Period 2, module 4 at Campus Norrköping; HT2026 instances currently marked CANCELLED on Studieinfo.

Exam_Format: LAB1 XR labs (4 ECTS) plus MUN1 oral examination (2 ECTS).
Summary: Introduces XR/VR/AR principles, human factors and interaction techniques with access to VR equipment and an oral design exam.
Key_Topics: Perception and multisensory considerations; XR display systems and tracking; Egocentric interaction and haptics; Audio interaction; Implementation of immersive applications
Notes: Period 2, module 4 at Campus Norrköping.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written exam (4.5 ECTS) plus UPG1 hand-in assignments (1.5 ECTS).
Summary: Covers Internet internals and optical backbone technologies, including TCP/IP protocols, optical fiber systems, coding and ISP economics.
Key_Topics: TCP/IP architecture; IPv4/IPv6 routing and mobility; DHCP and virtual circuits; UDP/TCP mechanisms; Optical fibers, WDM and components; Source/channel coding for packet networks; DNS, multicast, P2P; Ethernet setup and traffic analysis
Notes: Period 2, module 1.

Exam_Format: TEN1 written exam (4 ECTS) plus LAB1 labs (2 ECTS).
Summary: Network science course modelling complex networks and diffusion, combining theory with Matlab/Python labs.
Key_Topics: Graph representations and motifs; Laplacian properties; Centrality measures (PageRank, hubs/authorities); Community detection (Kernighan–Lin, Girvan–Newman, spectral); Network formation models (random, preferential attachment, small-world); Cascades and diffusion models; Graph learning/signal processing intro
Notes: Period 2, module 2.

Exam_Format: UPG1 written hand-ins (4 ECTS) plus UPG2 seminars (2 ECTS) graded Pass/Fail.
Summary: Scientific methods course for CS students covering literature analysis, method selection, ethics and professional research pathways ahead of the thesis.
Key_Topics: Quantitative/qualitative methods; Scientific writing; Literature search and referencing; Reliability/validity assessment; Ethics and societal impact; Professional research pathways in CS
Notes: Period 2, module 3; cannot be combined with TNM107 toward the degree.
